user.title: Connect to INIM Prime
user.description: Enter the connection details for your INIM PrimeX panel.

data_description.name: Friendly name for this panel — used for the device and entity names (e.g. "Allarme").
data_description.local_6004_password: The panel's LAN/programming password (default 'pass'). Required: read over the local protocol (TCP 6004) for multi-active scenes and the precise model. PrimeX 4.x firmware only.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate INIM Prime
reauth_confirm.description: The panel at {host} rejected the stored API key. Enter a valid API key to continue.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure INIM Prime
reconfigure.description: Update the connection details for your INIM PrimeX panel. Use this to rotate the API key or move the panel to a new address without removing the integration.

data_description.name: Friendly name for this panel — used for the device and entity names (e.g. "Allarme").
data_description.local_6004_password: The panel's LAN/programming password (default 'pass'). Required: read over the local protocol (TCP 6004) for multi-active scenes and the precise model. PrimeX 4.x firmware only.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the panel.
error.local_cannot_connect: Could not reach the panel's local protocol (TCP 6004). Check the LAN password and that the panel is reachable.
error.local_unsupported_firmware: This panel's firmware is not a supported PrimeX 4.x layout.

init.title: INIM Prime options
init.description: Tune the polling intervals and enable push mode to receive realtime local webhook events from the panel. The webhook URL is shown in a notification after saving.

data_description.label_language: Language used to guess each zone's device class (icon) from its name. Auto follows Home Assistant's language.
data_description.group_by_room: Place each zone (and its bypass switch) under a per-room device, guessed from the zone name. You then assign each room device to a Home Assistant area.

output_code_not_allowed.message: Output control requires the panel Code to be configured.

zones_not_ready.message: The command was rejected because one or more zones are not ready (open or in fault).

forced_arm_bad_target.message: Entity {entity_id} is not an INIM Prime entity.

forced_arm_single_target.message: Forced arm accepts exactly one target entity.

forced_arm_mode_with_scenario.message: Provide either a scenario or an arm mode, not both.

forced_arm_area_or_scenario.message: Target an area (with a mode), or provide a scenario name.

forced_arm_unbypassable_zones.message: Cannot force-arm: these zones can't be bypassed and are still open: {zones}.

event_log_bad_target.message: Entity {entity_id} is not an INIM Prime entity.

event_log_single_target.message: Get event log accepts exactly one target entity.

get_event_log.name: Get event log
get_event_log.description: Read the panel's stored event log over the local protocol (read-only) and return it.

entity_id.name: Target
entity_id.description: Any INIM Prime entity — identifies the panel to read.

limit.name: Limit
limit.description: Return only the most recent N events (0 = all).

arm_forced.name: Arm (forced)
arm_forced.description: Arm an area or apply a scenario even when zones are open, by bypassing the open zones first.

entity_id.name: Target
entity_id.description: An INIM Prime area (alarm control panel) — identifies the panel.

scenario.name: Scenario
scenario.description: Scenario to apply, by name (e.g. Ins.Notte). Use instead of a mode.

mode.name: Arm mode
mode.description: Arm mode when the target is an area (ignored for a scenario).

allow_partial.name: Allow partial arm
allow_partial.description: Arm even if some open zones cannot be bypassed (reports which stayed open). Off = refuse if any cannot be bypassed.
